# maktub_passkey

> A Flutter plugin for the WebAuthn PRF (`hmac-secret`) extension. It derives a
> stable, per-credential 32-byte secret from a synced passkey and reproduces the
> same secret on the user's other synced devices (iOS 18+ / Android API 28+).
> Use it to re-derive an end-to-end-encryption key after a device change without
> server-side key escrow. It is NOT a passkey login/auth library.

## When to use

- USE when: you need a passkey-derived secret that reproduces across a user's
  synced devices (e.g. recover an E2EE key on a new phone).
- DO NOT use when: you need passkey sign-in/auth flows, device-bound hardware
  keys, or support below iOS 18 / Android API 28. Use a full WebAuthn stack for
  authentication.

## Install

```yaml
dependencies:
  maktub_passkey: 0.1.0-dev.3 # pre-release: pin exactly; `^` will not select it
```

## Required platform setup (calls fail without it)

Passkeys are domain-bound. `relyingPartyId` must be a registrable HTTPS domain
(no scheme/port) that matches:
- iOS: Associated Domains entitlement `webcredentials:example.com` + an
  `apple-app-site-association` file with `{"webcredentials":{"apps":["TEAMID.bundleId"]}}`.
- Android: `/.well-known/assetlinks.json` with relation
  `delegate_permission/common.get_login_creds`, your package name, and the
  SHA-256 of every signing key (including the Play App Signing key).

## API (entry point: `MaktubPasskey`)

- `Future<PrfCapability> probePrf({required String relyingPartyId})`
  - Never throws; fails closed to `unavailable`.
  - `PrfCapability`: `prfSupported`, `backupEligible`, `backupState`, and the
    derived getter `recoverable` (true only if all three hold).
- `Future<PasskeyCreation> create({required String relyingPartyId, required String relyingPartyName, required String userName, required Uint8List userId, required Uint8List challenge})`
  - Creates a passkey with PRF enabled at creation (PRF cannot be added later).
  - Throws `MaktubPasskeyException` on failure.
  - `PasskeyCreation`: `credentialId` (base64url String), `attestationObject`
    (Uint8List), `capability` (PrfCapability).
- `Future<PasskeyAssertion> assertWithPrf({required String relyingPartyId, required Uint8List challenge, required Uint8List prfSalt, String? credentialId})`
  - Signs and evaluates PRF with `prfSalt` (your fixed 32-byte salt).
  - Omit `credentialId` for a discoverable assertion (user picks the passkey);
    pass it for a targeted one.
  - Throws `MaktubPasskeyException`.
  - `PasskeyAssertion`: `prfOutput` (Uint8List? — 32 bytes, null if PRF
    unavailable), `signature`, `authenticatorData`, `clientDataJson`,
    `credentialId` (String? — the credential the platform actually used),
    `userHandle` (String? — null when the platform returns none, common for a
    targeted assertion), `backupEligible` (bool), `backupState` (bool).

## Core invariants

- Determinism: the same `(credential, prfSalt)` always yields the same 32-byte
  `prfOutput`. Feed it through a KDF (e.g. HKDF-SHA256) for your context keys.
- Fail-closed recoverability: a derived secret only reproduces on a new device if
  the credential itself syncs there. ALWAYS gate on `PrfCapability.recoverable`
  (PRF supported AND `backupEligible` AND `backupState`) before relying on a key.
  A device-bound credential (`backupEligible == false`) is treated as NOT
  recoverable even though PRF works locally.

## Minimal example

```dart
import 'package:maktub_passkey/maktub_passkey.dart';

final pk = MaktubPasskey();
const rpId = 'example.com';

final cap = await pk.probePrf(relyingPartyId: rpId);
if (!cap.recoverable) return; // no PRF, or credential not synced

final a = await pk.assertWithPrf(
  relyingPartyId: rpId,
  challenge: challengeBytes, // 32 random bytes
  prfSalt: saltBytes,        // your fixed 32-byte salt
);
final Uint8List? secret = a.prfOutput; // 32 bytes, or null
```

## Testing

`package:maktub_passkey/testing.dart` ships `FakeMaktubPasskey`, a deterministic
test-only backend. Two fakes with the same `syncedSeed` model two synced devices
(same seed + same salt -> identical PRF). It asserts it is never run in a release
build. Set `MaktubPasskeyPlatform.instance = FakeMaktubPasskey(syncedSeed: seed)`
in tests and restore it in `tearDown`.

- pub.dev: https://pub.dev/packages/maktub_passkey

- Changelog: https://github.com/bytesbrains/maktub-passkey/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
